name: Byodo-In Temple
tagline: A full-scale replica of a 900-year-old Kyoto temple tucked against the Ko'olau cliffs — ring the 3-ton bell for blessings.
bestTime: Early morning for misty mountain backdrop and fewer crowds — golden hour light is stunning for photos
kamaaina: Kamaaina rate: $4 adults, $3 seniors, $2 kids (vs $7/$6/$4 general) with Hawaii ID. No cash — card/contactless only
talkStory: This 1968 replica of the 950-year-old Byodo-In in Uji, Japan was built entirely without nails using traditional Japanese joinery to honor a century of Japanese immigration to Hawaii. The 3-ton brass bon-sho bell at the entrance was cast in Osaka — ring it before entering to purify your mind. The 18-foot Amida Buddha inside is covered in gold lacquer and is among the largest carved wooden Buddhas outside Japan. Filming location for Lost, Hawaii Five-0, and Magnum P.I. — the surrounding Valley of the Temples is an active multi-faith cemetery, so be respectful.
